comment: Allow-list for known/expected patterns in test logs that should NOT cause build failures. Patterns are regex strings (case-insensitive). Each entry MUST have a 'pattern' (>= 10 chars, no bare .* or .+ wildcards) and a 'reason' explaining why it is safe. Overly broad patterns are rejected at scanner startup.

entries.pattern: Error loading translations - starting up anyway
entries.reason: Expected sentinel startup warning when translations are absent in the test environment
Integration tests in tests/integration/api/ deliberately trigger 500s as part of failure-path coverage
Login integration tests intentionally test malformed/bad credentials and expect this error
Test teardown occasionally surfaces unhandled promise rejections from deliberately-failed test cases
Services may briefly log ECONNREFUSED while waiting for CouchDB to become available during test startup

CouchDB OS-process debug log of _auth design-doc processing (validate_doc_update / map_doc) on user docs; the password field is hashed by CouchDB before storage and only appears in debug-level logs during tests
CouchDB OS-process debug log of translation-doc mapping; language metadata fields (code, name) are falsely detected as credential keys
Requests logged by the setup-phase morgan middleware (api/src/services/setup/router.js) before the API is fully initialised; 502/503 responses are expected during container boot

Sentinel transition processing errors that occur when e2e tests create or modify docs that are in a partial/inconsistent state during setup or teardown (shared-libs/transitions/src/transitions/index.js)

Individual transition handler errors logged by Sentinel during WebDriver tests; specific transitions (e.g. create_user_for_contacts) may error when test fixtures don't satisfy all transition preconditions (shared-libs/transitions/src/transitions/index.js)

entries.pattern: Error occurred when connecting to upgrade service:
entries.reason: The upgrade service is not running during normal WebDriver test jobs; transient connection failures are expected (api/src/services/setup/utils.js)
Node.js internal network stack frame from connection retries during startup; appears when services probe endpoints that are not yet listening
